summary: Surah Al-Muzzammil commands the Prophet to stand for night prayer, reciting the Qur’an slowly, as a weighty word will be laid upon him. It calls for patience with deniers, cites Pharaoh’s fate, and depicts the upheaval of the Last Day. The closing verse lightens the ruling: pray and recite what is easy, maintain prayer and charity, lend a goodly loan to Allah, and seek forgiveness—recognizing people’s illness, travel, and striving in Allah’s cause.
